svg.title: Drone interception production targets and communication APIs
svg.desc: Two-panel architecture diagram. The upper panel shows in-process ports and five Fast DDS topic flows among observer, console, and interceptor processes. The lower panel shows all sixteen production CMake targets, their direct dependencies, and the principal APIs used at each boundary.
svg.text: Drone interception — production targets & communication APIs
16 project targets · exact runtime topic contracts · direct CMake dependencies

g.text: Core work crosses explicit ports inside each process; every cross-process message crosses a Fast DDS Topic.
The radar and DDS implementations can be replaced without changing observer_core.
DDS receive results update projections on the console-owned polling loop.
Deterministic core decisions stay independent of Fast DDS and vehicle hardware.
Fast DDS data space · five cross-process contracts
writer target → Topic + generated wire type → reader target(s)
2 · Production CMake targets and direct API dependencies
Every arrow is a direct target_link_libraries edge from the consumer to the provider. API labels name the boundary actually used.
Scope: production targets only. Test executables and process_test_support are intentionally omitted. This diagram does not advance ROADMAP step 48.
